chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No. D- 464 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case. 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 16.04.2025. Mr. Ghulam Abbass Kuber, AAG. *********** None present for petitioner, nor any intimation received. Same was position on the last two consecutive dates of hearing which reflects that petitioner and his counsel have lost interest to proceed with the matter. Accordingly instant Constitutional Petition is dismissed for non-prosecution. JUDGE JUDGE Irfan/PA

Accordingly instant Constitutional Petition is dismissed for non-prosecution. JUDGE JUDGE Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Acquittal Appeal No. S- 86 of 2021 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case. 1. For orders on MA No.4061/2021 2. For hearing of main case. 22.04.2022. None present. Same was position on the last date of hearing. It appears that the instant Crl. Acquittal Appeal was presented on 17-07-2021, thereafter the appellant or his counsel have not pursued it diligently, which shows that appellant and his counsel have lost their interest; resultnatly the instant Crl. Acquittal Appeal stands dismissed for non-prosecution. Judge Irfan/PA

Acquittal Appeal stands dismissed for non-prosecution. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Appeal No. S- 132 of 2010 Criminal Appeal No. S- 51 of 2012 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main case. 18.04.2022. Mr. Qurban Ali Malano, Advocate for appellants. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Shar, Additional P.G. -.-.- Appellant is present on bail. Mr. Malano counsel for appellant requests for time as he is unwell. Request is allowed and the matter is adjourned to 25.04.2022 at 9.30 a.m. Office is directed to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned matter. Judge Irfan/PA

Office is directed to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned matter. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Bail Application No. S- 127 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of bail application. 18.04.2022. Mr. Muhammad Shakeel Lakho Advocate for Applicant. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Shar, Additional P.G. -.-.- Applicant/accused Muhammad Mithal Lakho is present on interim pre-arrest bail whereas Mr. Ameer Hussain Solangi Advocate files vakalatnama on behalf of complainant Naveed Ali which is taken on record. He requests time to produce ledger book of sale and purchase of wheat. At his request matter is adjourned to 16.05.2022. In the meanwhile trial Court is directed to examine the material witnesses and submit progress report on the next date of hearing. Judge Irfan/PA

In the meanwhile trial Court is directed to examine the material witnesses and submit progress report on the next date of hearing. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Appeal No. S- 05 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For Directions. 18.04.2022. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Shar, Additional P.G. -.-.-. None present for appellants, no intimation received. For orders on office objection at Flag ‘A’. 2. For orders on MA 1525/2022 (Ex) 3. For hearing of main case. 18.04.2022. None present for applicant, no intimation received. Same was position on the last date of hearing. It appears that either the alleged detenues have been recovered and the grievance of the applicant has been redressed or the applicant has lost interest to pursue with the Criminal Misc. Application. Accordingly, the same is dismissed for non-prosecution. Judge Irfan/PA

Accordingly, the same is dismissed for non-prosecution. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P No. S-28 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main case. 14.04.2022. Mr. Rukhsar Ahmed Junejo, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. Saeed Ahmed Wassan, AAG. -.-.- Inspector Javed Iqbal on behalf of S.S.P Sukkur files statement along with annexures, which is taken on record. As per contents of the statement, the police officials nominated in FIR No.98 of 2021 under sections 452, 364, 302 PPC are on interim pre-arrest bail granted by learned 2nd Additional Sessions Judge Sukkur on 01.04.2022. After going through the statement and annexures, counsel for petitioner has shown his satisfaction and does not press the instant constitutional petition, which is accordingly dismissed as not pressed. Judge Irfan/PA

After going through the statement and annexures, counsel for petitioner has shown his satisfaction and does not press the instant constitutional petition, which is accordingly dismissed as not pressed. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Misc. Application No. S- 116 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of case. 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. 14.04.2022. Mr. Ghulamullah Memon, Advocate for applicant. -.-.- Mr. Abdul Sattar G. Luhrani Advocate files vakalatnama on behalf of respondent No.1 which is taken on record. He requests time to prepare his brief. At his request matter is adjourned to 28.04.2022. Judge Irfan/PA

At his request matter is adjourned to 28.04.2022. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P No. S- 59 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case. 1. For orders on office objection at Flag ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 07.04.2022. Mr. Khan Muhammad Sangi, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Shehriyar Imdad Awan, Assistant Advocate General Sindh along with DSP Abdul Razak Bugti SDPO Kandiyaro and SIP Ali Akber SHO of Police Station Kandiaryo. -.-.- The written statements filed on behalf of official respondents Nos. 2 to 4 are unsatisfactory. On the last date of hearing a detailed order was passed in which certain allegations were made by the petitioner against SHO Ali Akber Khokhar of Police Station Kandiyaro but S.S.P failed to conduct enquiry against him. In such circumstances S.S.P Naushehro Feroze is directed to conduct enquiry against SIP Ali Akber Khokhar SHO of Police Station Kandiyaro and submit report on the next date of hearing. Today SDPO Abdul Razak Bugti SDPO Kandiyaro is present and requests for date as S.S.P Naushehro Feroze is unwell. Mr. Khan Muhammad Sangi Advocate appearing on behalf of petitioner submits that still SHO of Police Station Kandiyaro is issuing threats to the petitioner for committing his half fry case and has refused to return his motorcycle. In the view of above, S.S.P Naushehro Feroze is directed to provide protection to the petitioner and his family members and to ensure that no harm shall be caused to the petitioner by SHO of Police Station Kandiaro. It is made clear that if any harm is caused to petitioner then S.S.P Naushehro Feroze shall himself be responsible. S.S.P Naushehro Feroze and SHO Police Station Kandiyaro shall appear in person on the next date of hearing along with enquiry report.

It is made clear that if any harm is caused to petitioner then S.S.P Naushehro Feroze shall himself be responsible. S.S.P Naushehro Feroze and SHO Police Station Kandiyaro shall appear in person on the next date of hearing along with enquiry report. Office is directed to provide copy of this order to Additional Advocate General Sindh as well as fax to S.S.P Naushehro Feroze today for information and compliance. To come up on 11.04.2022 at 11.00 a.m. Judge Irfan/PA

To come up on 11.04.2022 at 11.00 a.m. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Acquittal Appeal No. S-42 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main case. 07.04.2022. Mr. Ghulam Mujtaba Jakhar, Advocate for Appellant. Mr. Munawar Ali Shaikh, Advocate for respondent No.3. -.-.- Mr. Saeed Jamal Lund Advocate files vakalatnama on behalf f respondent No.1 Nadeem while Mr. Waseem Shahbaz Shar files vaklatnama on behalf of respondent No.2 which are taken on record. Report has also been filed on behalf of SHO of Police Station Sobhodero which is also taken on record. At this juncture, appellant/complainant Miss Aliza daughter of Irshad Hattar submits that still the respondents/ accused persons are threatening her and she is unable to pass her regular life. The respondents are present in Court and they are warned to be careful in future and not to cause any kind of harassment to the appellant/complainant while SHO of Police Station Sobhodero is directed to provide protection to the appellant/complainant, if any harm is caused to the appellant/complainant Miss Aliza daughter of Irshad bycaste Hattar, then SHO of Police Station Sobhodero shall be responsible for all consequences. Adjourned to 21.04.2022. Judge Irfan/PA

Adjourned to 21.04.2022. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Misc. Syed Sardar Ali Shah, Additional P.G. -.-.- Learned counsel for applicant submits that now the matter is fixed by Hon’ble Supreme Court as such requests for time. Time is allowed. Adjourned to a date in office. Judge Irfan/PA

Adjourned to a date in office. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Misc. Application is dismissed for non-prosecution. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Misc. Haji Shamsuddin Rajper, Advocate for applicants. -.-.-.-.-.-.- After arguing the matter at length, learned counsel for applicants does not press the instant Criminal Misc. Application and submits that the applicants will avail the remedy in accordance with law before the trial Court, which is accordingly dismissed as not pressed. Judge Irfan/PA

Application and submits that the applicants will avail the remedy in accordance with law before the trial Court, which is accordingly dismissed as not pressed. To come up on 25.04.2022. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Bail Application No.S-64 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of Bail Application. 07.04.2022. Mr. Abdul Ghafoor Jiskani, Advocate for applicant/accused. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Shar, Additional Prosecutor General. -.-.-.-.-.-.- Applicant/accused is present on interim pre-arrest bail whereas complainant Pervaiz Ahmed Shar is called absent. In the interest of justice adjourned to 25.04.2022. Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P No. D- 171 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of case. 1. For orders on office/objection at Flag ‘A’. 2. For orders on CMA No. 783/2022 (Ex) 3. For hearing of main case. 05.04.2022. Mr. Khan Muhammad Sangi Advocate/Petitioner present in person. Mr. Muhammad Junaid Akram Malik, Advocate for respondent No.10. Mr. Mumtaz Ali Gopang, Assistant Attorney General along with Akhlaque Nabi Dayo Project Director M-6 and Mr. Muhammad Arshad Deputy Driector (Legal). -.-.-.-.-.-.- The case of petitioner is that Government of Pakistan and Government of China entered in the agreement for rebuilt of economy of Pakistan and given the name as China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) which is a collection of infrastructure projects that are under construction throughout Pakistan beginning in 2013 at that time the original value was $47 billion, the value of CPEC projects is worth $62 billion as of 2020. As per short term Project the Sukkur to Hyderabad Motorway was to be constructed and the cost of project prepared in the year 2016-2017. At the relevant time the cost of un-approved project was shown Rs.163000.000 and the record reflects that Sukkur- Hyderabad motorway showing land acquisition approved in the year 2014. In the year 2018 National Highway Authority Pakistan (NHA) invited proposal regarding the design, Traffic studies, Commercial Feasibility Study and Bid Evaluation/Bid Comparative Analysis for Hyderabad-Sukkur Motorway project on Built-Operate-Transfer (BOT) Basis under Public Private Partnership (PPP) Arrangement. Learned counsel /Petitioner also submits that due to non-construction of motorway, the National Highway Authority also not repairing the road leading from Sukkur to Hyderabad which is presently in worst condition and the people/public at large is suffering badly and there is

In the year 2018 National Highway Authority Pakistan (NHA) invited proposal regarding the design, Traffic studies, Commercial Feasibility Study and Bid Evaluation/Bid Comparative Analysis for Hyderabad-Sukkur Motorway project on Built-Operate-Transfer (BOT) Basis under Public Private Partnership (PPP) Arrangement. Learned counsel /Petitioner also submits that due to non-construction of motorway, the National Highway Authority also not repairing the road leading from Sukkur to Hyderabad which is presently in worst condition and the people/public at large is suffering badly and there is big cry in the public at large, when NHA is collecting Toll tax from the vehicles. The respondents have not started the construction of Motorway Sukkur to Hyderabad M-6 and National Highway Authority invited another Bid in the year 2020 but no fruitful result has neither come on record nor the respondents started any development for construction of Sukkur-Hyderabad Motorway. After institution of the matter, the notices were issued to the respondents as well as DAG. On last date of hearing viz. 08.03.2022 Project Director M-6 appeared who filed certain documents and he has assured that required work will be started at the site as soon as possible. However, the Court noted that earlier this project was halted due to unknown reasons and it is again started but as a result whereof the cost of the Project has enormously increased. Today Akhlauqe Nabi Dayo Project Director M-6 and Muhammad Arshad Deputy Director (Legal) are present and state that now the total cost of the project is 308,194 Millions. It is surprising to note here that in between 2016-17 and year 2022 the difference of the cost is 145194 Million and in such circumstances the loss has occurred in the project near about 145194. The statement filed today by respondent No.7 is not satisfactory because no reason has been shown in the statement that why the project has been delayed. In the circumstances, let notice be issued to Respondent No.2 (Secretary of Communication) Government of Pakistan Islamabad and Respondent No.7 (Chairman National Highway Authority Islamabad) who are directed to appear in person on the next date along with their comprehensive reports.

The statement filed today by respondent No.7 is not satisfactory because no reason has been shown in the statement that why the project has been delayed. In the circumstances, let notice be issued to Respondent No.2 (Secretary of Communication) Government of Pakistan Islamabad and Respondent No.7 (Chairman National Highway Authority Islamabad) who are directed to appear in person on the next date along with their comprehensive reports. However, notice shall also be issued to Director General NAB Sukkur. To come up on 13.04.2022 at 11.00 a.m. Judge Judge Irfan/PA

To come up on 13.04.2022 at 11.00 a.m. Judge Judge Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Misc. Application No. S- 767 of 2021 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case. 4. For orders on office/objection at Flag ‘A’. 5. For orders on MA No.6590/2021 (Ex) 6. For hearing of main case. 01.04.2022. Mr. Mehfooz Ahmed Awan, Advocate for Applicant. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- After arguing at length learned counsel for the applicant submits that he will not press the instant Criminal Misc. Application and will file direct complaint before the Court of law having jurisdiction. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Shar, learned Additional P.G present in Court in some other cases, waived notice of this Criminal Misc. Application and raised no objection. In view of the above, instant Criminal Misc. Application is disposed of. However, learned counsel for the applicant is at liberty to file direct complaint before the Court of law having jurisdiction. J U D G E Irfan/PA

However, learned counsel for the applicant is at liberty to file direct complaint before the Court of law having jurisdiction. J U D G E Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Bail Application No. S- 90 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of Bail Application. 1. For orders on office objection at Flag ‘A’. 2. For hearing of Bail Application. 31.03.2022. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, D.P.G -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Mr. Alam Sher Bozdar Advocate present and states that accused has got taken away his file and he is no more an advocate on behalf of applicant/accused. He submits that his name may be deleted from filer cover as well as cause list. Order accordingly. Since no one is in attendance on behalf of applicant/accused and no intimation is received. It appears that applicant has lost interest in the matter and he does not want to proceed the matter, as such the instant bail application is dismissed for non-prosecution. J U D G E Irfan/PA

It appears that applicant has lost interest in the matter and he does not want to proceed the matter, as such the instant bail application is dismissed for non-prosecution. J U D G E Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Jail Appeal No. S- 62 of 2021 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For Directions. 24.03.2022. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, D.P.G. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- This Criminal Jail Appeal has been filed by appellant through Senior Superintendent Central Prison & Correctional Facility Hyderabad and the office was directed to prepare paper book on State expenses. Since accused is in Jail and as per office note till yet paper book costs has not been deposited as such office of D.A.G is directed to pay the required costs for preparation of paper book. After preparation of paper book the matter shall be listed after notice to all concerned. J U D G E Irfan/PA

After preparation of paper book the matter shall be listed after notice to all concerned. J U D G E Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Revision Application No. S-84 of 2019 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main case. 24.03.2022. Mr. Shabbir Ali Bozdar, Advocate for applicants. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, D.P.G. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Applicants are present. One Muhammad Shareef who claims to be nephew of complainant is present and requests for a date on the ground that his counsel is busy before another Court of this bench. At his request matter is adjourned to 25.04.2022. J U D G E Irfan/PA

At his request matter is adjourned to 25.04.2022. J U D G E Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Jail Appeal No. S-91 of 2019 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main case. 24.03.2022. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, D.P.G. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Mr. Muhammad Zohaib Azam Advocate was appointed as an advocate for pauper accused/appellant Allah Dino alias Dino Bhatti on State expenses but today he is called absent. Record reflects that on last so many dates of hearing he is not appearing to proceed with the matter. In view of above, Mr. Shabbir Ali Bozdar Advocate is appointed as an advocate for pauper accused/appellant Allah Dino alias Dino Bhatti on State expenses on payment of Rs. 30,000/- (thirty thousand) as professional fee which will be paid by the office of Prosecutor General Sindh. Office is also directed to supply the copy of paper book to advocate for pauper accused for preparation. Office shall send copy of this order to Prosecutor General Sindh at Karachi by fax for information and compliance. Adjourned to a date in office. J U D G E Irfan/PA

Adjourned to a date in office. J U D G E Irfan/PA ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Jail Appeal No. S-128 of 2019 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on MA No.6937/2021 (U/S 345 Cr.P.C 2. For orders on MA No.6958/2021 (Permission to Compromise) 3. For orders on MA No.5815/2019 (426 Cr.P.C) 4. For orders on MA No.5398/2019 (Suspension) 5. For hearing of main case. 24.03.2022. Mr. Fayyaz Ahmed Rajper, Advocate for appellants. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, D.P.G. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Learned counsel for appellants submits that the legal heirs of the deceased appeared before the Court of learned Ist. Additional Sessions Judge (MCTC) Ghotki for recording of their statements but the Court had informed that Court had already sent the relevant papers to this Court as such requests that learned trial Court may be directed to record the statements of legal heirs namely Allah Rakhio and Mst. Aghi, the real father and mother of deceased Mst. Zareena. As such office is directed to re-send both the applications to learned trial Court for recording statements of Allah Rakhio and Mst. Aghi legal heirs of deceased and after recording of their statements the trial Court shall submit fresh report regarding legal heirs of deceased and compromise arrived at between the parties. To come up on 11.04.2022. J U D G E Irfan/PA

On the last date of hearing viz. 11.02.2022 following order was passed. “ None present for the appellant and same was the position on the last date of hearing. In the interest of justice matter is adjourned to a date in office with note of caution that if learned counsel for the appellant faisl to appear or avoids proceeding with the matter on the next date, then matter shall be heard and decided in accordance with law.” Today position is the same, neither the appellant/complainant is present nor his counsel is in attendance and no intimation received which shows that either the appellant/complainant has settled his dispute with the private respondents outside of this Court or he has no interest to pursue the matter, resultantly instant Criminal acquittal Appeal is dismissed for non-prosecution.
